Whatever methods are used, it is useless to fight against God's judgments. The more we enjoy the pleasures of this life, the more we make ourselves unfit for the troubles of this life. The Chaldean army will break into the land of Judah, and in a short time destroy everything. The day is coming when those who are careless and secure in sinful ways will be punished. It is foolish to waste time when we have eternal salvation to work out and enemies of that salvation to fight against. But they were eager, not so that they might fulfill God's plans, but so that they might fill their own treasures; yet God used this for his own purposes. The corrupt heart of a person, in its natural state, casts out evil thoughts, just as a fountain casts out water. It is always flowing, yet always full. The merciful God is reluctant to leave even a provoking people, and urges them that by repentance and reformation they may prevent things from reaching an extreme.

When the Lord arises to take vengeance, no sinners of any age or rank, or of either sex escape. They were set upon the world and completely carried away by love for it. If we judge this sin by God's word, we find many in every station and rank given over to it. Those who flatter us in a sinful way are our worst and most dangerous enemies. Oh that people would be wise for their souls! Ask for the old paths; the way of godliness and righteousness has always been the way God has owned and blessed. Ask for the old paths set forth by the written word of God. When you have found the good way, continue in it; you will find abundant reward at your journey's end. But if people will not obey the voice of God and flee to his appointed Refuge, it will be clear at the day of judgment that they are ruined because they reject God's word.

God rejects their outward services as worthless to atone for their sins. Sacrifice and incense were meant to direct them to a Mediator; but when offered to purchase permission to continue in sin, they provoke God. The sins of God’s professing people make them an easy target for their enemies. They dare not show themselves. Saints may rejoice in hope of God's mercies, even though they see them only in the promise: sinners must mourn because of fear of God's judgments, even though they see them only in the threats. They are the worst of rebels and are all corrupters. Sinners soon become tempters. They are like ore that is thought to have good metal in it, but which turns out to be worthless dross. Nothing will prevail to separate them from their sins. They will be called rejected silver, useless and worthless. When warnings, corrections, rebukes, and all means of grace leave people unchanged, they will be left, as rejected by God, to everlasting misery. Let us pray that we may be refined by the Lord, just as silver is refined.
